summary: workspace-config-loader exfiltrates host and environment data to a hardcoded endpoint
details: lib/trap-core.js loads fs, os, https, and dns modules and assembles a payload containing host identifiers (os.hostname, os.platform, os.homedir, process.env.USER, process.cwd) which it transmits via https.request/https.get POST calls to a hardcoded remote endpoint (lib/trap-core.js lines 267, 274, 292-293, 346). The file also probes the filesystem for existence of paths (fs.existsSync at lines 26, 79, 194) and runs a ping reachability check (line 38) before exfiltration. The package name 'workspace-config-loader' implies a benign configuration utility, but the bundled trap-core module is a system-information beacon: it reads identifying environment data and ships it off-host to an attacker-controlled endpoint. There is no documented purpose for any 'workspace config loader' to POST hostname, platform, username, and working directory to a remote server.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
